content:
# Perf v3 Render Speed — dorea grade (2026-04-03)

## Test File
- **Input**: `footage/raw/2025-11-01/DJI_20251101111428_0055_D.MP4`
- **Resolution**: 3840x2160 @ 119.88fps, 14.0s duration, 1671 frames, 218MB

## Output
- **File**: `working/graded/DJI_20251101111428_0055_D_graded_perfv3.mp4`
- **Codec**: H.264 (libx264, High 4:4:4 Predictive, gbrp) + AAC audio passthrough
- **Size**: 198MB

## Render Speed
- **Total wall time**: 3m 33.1s
- **Calibration phase** (RAUNE-Net + Depth Anything on 20 keyframes): ~46s
- **Per-frame grading** (1671 frames): ~2m 47s
- **Frame rate**: ~10.0 fps (100 frames per ~10s)
- **Realtime ratio**: 0.066x (14s video / 213s processing)
- **CPU time**: 10m 12s user, 1m 3s sys

## Pipeline Config
- **Binary**: dorea-cli @ commit 135f8df (perf v3: temporal interp + fused LAB + raw RGB IPC + HF bypass)
- **Proxy size**: 518px (default) — frames downscaled before depth inference
- **RAUNE-Net**: loaded from default path (weights_95.pth), ran on CUDA during calibration
- **Depth Anything V2**: CUDA, keyframe-only inference at proxy resolution
- **Grading**: CUDA (LUT + HSL + fused depth-aware ambiance+warmth)
- **Temporal interpolation**: ON (MSE threshold 0.005, max interval 12)
- **Encoder**: libx264 (NVENC not available in this container)
- **GPU**: RTX 3060 6GB VRAM

## Comparison vs Baseline (commit 7970229)

| Metric | Baseline | Perf v3 | Speedup |
|--------|----------|---------|---------|
| Total wall time | 38m 0.6s | 3m 33.1s | **10.7x** |
| Calibration | ~2m 53s | ~46s | 3.8x |
| Per-frame grading | ~35m 7s | ~2m 47s | **12.6x** |
| Throughput | 0.74 fps | ~10.0 fps | **13.5x** |
| Realtime ratio | 0.006x | 0.066x | 11x |
| CPU user time | 41m 39s | 10m 12s | 4.1x |
| CPU sys time | 4m 0s | 1m 3s | 3.8x |

## Bottleneck Shift
Per-frame depth inference is no longer the bottleneck — temporal interpolation skips ~83% of frames (at max_interval=12 on 120fps content). Remaining cost is dominated by ffmpeg decode/encode and keyframe depth inference. Next potential speedups: NVENC encoding, binary IPC (eliminate base64), pre-allocated scratch buffers for lerp.

## Quality
Visual comparison (source vs graded at t=2s, 5s, 10s) shows proper color restoration: blue/cyan cast removed, reef warm tones recovered, depth-aware ambiance providing foreground/background separation. No visible interpolation artifacts on non-keyframe frames. Quantitative PSNR/SSIM pending (--no-depth-interp reference run in progress).
